Strategic Framework

Positioning in the AI Era

How consulting firms can reposition their service offering around AI-native methodologies — illustrated through concrete project examples.

Example Project: AI Transformation Initiative

A concrete illustration of what consulting firms can now bring to market

Project Type
AI Transformation
Focus Area
M&A Screening & Due Diligence
Technology
Generative AI

How Generative AI Can Improve M&A Screening and Due Diligence

An AI transformation initiative analyzing how Generative AI can fundamentally accelerate and enhance the M&A process — from early-stage target identification through to full due diligence. The engagement maps where AI creates the most leverage across the deal lifecycle and codifies those learnings into reusable methodology that a firm can deploy on every subsequent transaction.

The Opportunity

M&A processes are information-intensive and time-pressured — exactly the conditions where Generative AI creates the most value. Target screening traditionally requires analysts to manually review hundreds of companies across financials, news, filings, and market data. AI can compress weeks of research into hours, surface non-obvious targets, and generate structured summaries ready for senior review. Due diligence workstreams — commercial, financial, legal — similarly involve large volumes of documents that AI can rapidly synthesize, flag risks within, and benchmark against comparable deals.

Potential Deliverable: AI Prompt Blueprint & Methodology

The core output of this engagement is a practical, immediately deployable AI Prompt Blueprint — a structured methodology documenting which prompts produce the best results across each M&A workstream, which approaches should be avoided, and how to quality-check AI outputs before they reach a client. The blueprint covers three core areas:

  • Target Screening — prompts for identifying and prioritizing acquisition targets by sector, size, and strategic fit
  • Market Analysis — prompts for competitive landscape mapping, market sizing, and trend synthesis
  • Due Diligence — prompts for document review, risk flagging, management assessment, and comparable deal benchmarking
Why This Is a Compelling Consulting Proposition

This type of engagement exemplifies the new consulting model: rather than delivering a one-time PowerPoint, the firm delivers a reusable AI methodology that embeds into the client's deal team permanently. It demonstrates AI expertise with tangible, low-risk proof of value — and positions the firm as the natural partner for the broader AI transformation of the client's transaction practice.

It also addresses a real concern in AI adoption: knowing what not to do. Documenting which prompting approaches fail, hallucinate, or produce unreliable outputs is as valuable as the working blueprints themselves — and is something only a firm with structured methodology can credibly deliver.

Strategic Intelligence

AI Disruption Mechanism
in Consulting

How artificial intelligence is fundamentally restructuring the consulting industry's value chain — from workforce pyramids to client demand patterns.

The Reinforcing Cycle

A self-reinforcing disruption loop where each stage amplifies the next

The Four-Stage Disruption Loop

Customer AI adoption drives reduced consulting demand, which forces staffing cuts, which weakens firm competitiveness, which accelerates client self-reliance — a compounding cycle that is difficult to reverse once in motion.

From Scarcity to Overload

The fundamental shift in where consulting value is created

The Pyramid Shift

Structural transformation of consulting firm staffing models

What the Pyramid Inversion Means for Talent

The traditional consulting pyramid — built on a wide base of junior analysts and associates performing data collection, research, and templated analysis — was designed for an era of information scarcity. As AI automates the bulk of these entry-level tasks (building decks, gathering market data, writing first-draft reports), consulting firms will employ significantly fewer junior staff. The inverted pyramid that emerges reflects a future where senior judgment, client relationships, and strategic interpretation carry most of the value — while AI handles the analytical groundwork that once required entire analyst cohorts. This is not simply downsizing: it is a structural rebalancing of the consulting workforce toward roles that remain genuinely difficult for machines to replicate.

Real-World Case Studies — Nordics

Consulting Firms
in the AI Era

Concrete examples of how consulting firms are acting on AI — through deployments, partnerships, and acquisitions across the Nordic region.

Examples in Practice

What firms are actually doing, not just saying

Ekan Management 📍 Sweden 2 Initiatives
AI Strategy & Public Sector AI
AI Strategy 📅 2024 · 📍 Sweden
AI x Strategi (with Eghed)

Together with AI boutique Eghed, Ekan created the "AI x Strategi" offering using their AIRA methodology to help Swedish organisations move from AI planning to real execution — assessing technical, organisational, and business prerequisites for scaling AI programmes.[1]

Public Sector AI 📅 2024 · 📍 Sweden
AI for Information Management

Ekan helped a Swedish public-sector organisation streamline document and case handling using an AI RAG model, enabling efficient classification and governance of public information in line with Swedish archival law and GDPR.[2]

Signal: Specialist boutiques are productising AI strategy and deploying AI in regulated public-sector contexts — competing with larger firms on credibility and domain depth rather than brand.

BCG × Avinor 📅 2023–2024 📍 Norway AI Deployment
Optimizing Runway De-icing & Asset Maintenance

BCG worked with Avinor, Norway's state-owned airport operator, to build internal AI capabilities and deploy AI-supported systems for two operationally critical processes: runway de-icing and asset maintenance, moving beyond advisory into hands-on tool deployment alongside client operations teams.

Signal: Strategy firms are expanding into AI delivery — embedding with client teams to build and integrate operational tools, blurring the line between consulting and implementation.[3]

Accenture 📍 Nordics 3 Initiatives
Sovereign AI · GenAI Deployment · M&A
Sovereign AI 📅 2024 · 📍 Norway
Telenor AI Factory

Selected to jointly operate Telenor AI Factory's platform for sovereign and secure AI services, scaling high-performance AI compute and model-as-a-service for enterprise customers in Norway.[4]

GenAI 📅 2024 · 📍 Sweden
Telia

Built a GenAI solution automating governance of a large telecom data repository, driving efficiency through metadata automation across tens of thousands of data fields.[5]

M&A 📅 4 Mar 2025 · 📍 Denmark
Acquires Halfspace

Acquired Danish AI specialist Halfspace — 80 specialists, 100+ projects — to rapidly scale Nordic AI delivery capability and workflow automation.[6]

Signal: Accenture is building AI presence across the full stack in the Nordics — sovereign infrastructure, application deployment, and talent acquisition — cementing a broad, difficult-to-replicate position.

EY 📍 Sweden 2 Initiatives
AI Automation · AI Adoption Strategy
AI Automation 📅 2023–2024 · 📍 Sweden
Insurance Claims Automation

EY deployed its Fabric Document Intelligence platform at a Nordic insurer, resulting in near real-time claims processing with 70% of documents correctly extracted automatically — freeing agents for higher-value customer interactions.[7]

AI Adoption 📅 2024 · 📍 Sweden
Preem AI Strategy

EY facilitated an EY wavespace™ session in Madrid for ~20 of Preem's senior leaders — including CEO and CFO — generating 51 AI use cases for Sweden's largest fuel company, four of which were selected for immediate development as a proof of concept.[8]

Signal: EY is combining operational AI automation with C-suite AI strategy facilitation — demonstrating a broad capability set spanning both technical delivery and organisational transformation.

Deloitte 📍 Sweden 2 Initiatives
Ecosystem Partnership · AI Observability
Ecosystem 📅 2024 · 📍 Sweden
Partnership with AI Sweden

Deloitte partnered with AI Sweden — Sweden's national public-private AI initiative — to deepen AI collaboration in financial services and contribute to talent development across Sweden's broader AI ecosystem.[9]

AI Observability 📅 9 Jul 2025 · 📍 Sweden
Dynatrace Partnership

Deloitte Sverige entered a strategic partnership with Dynatrace to deliver AI-driven observability for hybrid and multi-cloud environments — helping organisations meet ISO 27001 and DORA compliance while maintaining cloud performance.[10]

Signal: Deloitte is simultaneously embedding into national AI ecosystems and building AI infrastructure partnerships — a dual strategy combining long-term credibility with near-term technical delivery.

Implement Consulting Group × Semler Gruppen 📅 Autumn 2024 📍 Denmark GenAI Adoption
Rolling Out Generative AI Adoption Across 1,600 Employees

Implement ran a full generative AI adoption programme for Semler Gruppen — Denmark's largest automotive group — targeting 1,600 employees across geographically dispersed locations with vastly different AI skill levels. The engagement designed tailored foundational AI training and Microsoft Copilot sessions, embedding a multilateral steering group with the CEO as sponsor, resulting in employees progressing from basic queries to complex AI-powered workflows within a short period.

Signal: Specialist change management firms are building a distinct AI adoption practice — demonstrating that successful AI transformation is as much about people and process as it is about technology.[11]

Pricing & Proposition Evolution

New Pricing
Strategies

How AI is fundamentally reshaping consulting economics — from pricing models and fee structures to the nature of the projects firms sell and deliver.

The Pricing Revolution

AI efficiency forces a structural rethink of consulting economics

Pricing Benchmarking

Real-world evidence of the pricing shift already underway at leading firms

McKinsey & Company 📅 Nov 2025 📍 Global Pricing Shift
From Billable Hours to Outcome-Based Fees
25%
of McKinsey's global fees now outcome-based
<20%
of McKinsey's business is now traditional strategy work
72%
of McKinsey staff are active users of Lilli, its AI tool

McKinsey has disclosed that a quarter of its global fees are now driven by outcomes-based pricing — a decisive break from the billable-hours model that has defined consulting economics for decades. Michael Birshan, McKinsey's UK managing partner, told reporters that clients are increasingly bypassing traditional scoping conversations entirely: rather than asking "what does this project cost?", they arrive with the outcome they want and tie compensation directly to whether McKinsey can deliver it. Some fixed cost components remain, but the direction of travel is clear.

This shift is directly enabled by AI. As McKinsey's internal tool Lilli compresses research and analysis time by up to 30%, the traditional justification for billing by the hour weakens — and clients are responding by demanding accountability for results rather than effort. Strategy work, once McKinsey's core offering, now represents less than 20% of the firm's business as clients turn instead to deep implementation support across data, AI, operations, and workforce transformation.

Signal: When the world's largest strategy firm publicly commits a quarter of its fee pool to outcome-based pricing, it sets a market benchmark that clients at every other firm will soon reference — accelerating the industry-wide shift away from billable hours.[1]

Project Portfolio Transformation

Yesterday's flagship engagements versus tomorrow's high-value mandates

The New ERP: AI Transformation
Legacy Flagship

ERP Transformation

Multi-year SAP/Oracle implementations. Process standardization. Change management. Large junior analyst teams building data migration playbooks and testing scripts.

→
New Flagship

AI Transformation

Enterprise-wide AI readiness assessment, use-case prioritization, model deployment, governance frameworks, and workforce reskilling — the defining large-scale engagement of the 2020s.[3]

📊

Strategy-as-a-Subscription

Rather than one-off project engagements, firms offer continuous intelligence platforms — AI-powered dashboards providing clients with ongoing market monitoring, competitive signals, and scenario updates. BCG and McKinsey are piloting retainer models built on proprietary AI tools.[4]

🤖

AI Governance & Compliance

As regulators introduce AI-specific compliance requirements (EU AI Act, SEC AI disclosures), a new advisory category emerges: AI risk assessment, model auditing, and governance framework design. The Big 4 are particularly well-positioned given their existing compliance practices.[5]

⚡

Rapid Diagnostics

AI enables firms to compress what was once a 4-week discovery phase into a 72-hour AI-powered assessment. Bain's AI Radar and similar tools allow consultants to deliver board-ready diagnostics at a fraction of traditional timelines — enabling new lower-cost entry products that were previously uneconomical.[6]

🏗️

Co-Build & Product Development

Consulting is evolving from pure advisory to co-building AI products with clients. BCG X exemplifies this: strategy and delivery merge into a single engagement where consultants embed with client engineering teams to build and launch AI-native products. Fees shift from day rates to equity and success-based structures.[7]

🧠

Change Management & Workforce AI Adoption

Ironically, one of AI's largest new consulting categories is helping organizations adapt to AI itself. Workforce upskilling programs, AI change management playbooks, and culture transformation engagements are growing rapidly — particularly for large enterprises facing employee resistance to AI tooling.

📋

AI Due Diligence (M&A)

Private equity and M&A transactions now require AI capability assessment as part of commercial and technical due diligence. Consultants evaluate target companies' AI infrastructure, data assets, model inventories, and competitive moats — a new diligence workstream driven by AI's role as a core value driver in acquisitions.[8]

The Consulting Offer in Transition

Key dimensions shifting from traditional to AI-native models

Dimension Traditional Model AI-Native Model
Pricing model Daily rate × team size × weeks Outcome-based / subscription / success fee
Flagship engagement ERP transformation (2–3 years) AI transformation & governance (6–18 months)
Discovery phase 3–6 week research sprint 72-hour AI diagnostic
Deliverable PowerPoint recommendations deck Live AI tool / platform / co-built product
Team composition Heavy on junior analysts Lean senior team + AI toolchain
Client relationship Episodic project mandate Continuous intelligence partner
